_risk_level_doc: Fix is bounded to 5 files (commands/commit.md, commands/push.md, and 3 Python scripts); no schema change, no auth boundary, no pipeline orchestrator. Low regression probability.

requirement.original: Fix two related script invocation bugs: (A) python command not found — replace python with python3 in commands/commit.md; (B) CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset — add fallback to CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID in scripts/write-commit-grant.py, scripts/execute-push.py, scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py.
requirement.clarified: Same as original — requirement is fully specified.
requirement.what: Replace bare `python` with `python3` in 3 bash code blocks in commit.md; update SESSION_ID assignment and abort prose in push.md lines 123-127 to use CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback; add CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID env var fallback in 3 Python scripts' session-id resolution paths
requirement.why: On this Ubuntu system `python` is not installed (only python3); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is never set (only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is). These cause /commit and /push to fail at runtime.

included: commands/push.md lines 123-127 — SESSION_ID fallback expression and abort prose update
scripts/execute-push.py main() step 2 lines 107-114 — CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback
excluded: commands/dev-command.md:695 — permission-string template containing python (not an actual invocation)
scripts/*.sh — bash scripts use different env resolution
Any other env var issues outside these five files

success_criteria: No bare `python ` token invokes scripts in commit.md bash code blocks
push.md SESSION_ID assignment uses fallback expression including CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID
All three scripts resolve session ID from CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset
Both vars unset → exit 2 (abort behavior preserved)
constraints: Do not change --sid CLI override behavior
Do not change shebang lines (already python3)
Do not touch files outside the five affected files listed in scope_boundary.in_user_path

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: commands/dev-command.md:695 — contains `python` in permission-string template; not an actual invocation

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Contains `python ~/.claude/scripts/todo/<script>.py` in a permission-string template (allowlist entry example, not an executable bash line). Not an invocation; does not fail at runtime. Recording for visibility.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: `/commit` step 5 exits 127 on `python` command; scripts abort with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Bug A: commit.md uses bare `python` which is not installed (Ubuntu 22.04+ ships only python3). Bug B: Scripts read CLAUDE_SESSION_ID but this system sets only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — configuration mismatch with system environment, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Scripts were authored on a system where `python` was symlinked to python3, or the distinction was not checked. CLAUDE_SESSION_ID vs CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID mismatch is a known env naming split in Claude Code.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: On systems without the `python` symlink, exit 127 blocks the entire /commit workflow. CLAUDE_SESSION_ID being unset causes scripts to abort before writing the grant/sentinel needed for git operations.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Present since scripts were written; not a recent regression
observations: `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` is empty/unset in runtime env (observed: Bash tool)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Grep on commands/commit.md confirmed three bare `python` invocations at lines 81, 89, 153. Read of each script confirmed CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is the sole env var checked with no fallback at write-commit-grant.py:122, execute-push.py:107, write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py:56.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression — configuration/env mismatch, no regression keywords in user requirement

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: python command not found — replace all python with python3 in command specs that invoke scripts
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: commands/commit.md contains no bare `python ` in bash code blocks that invoke scripts at /root/.claude/scripts/ or ${CONTROL_ROOT}/scripts/

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: fall back to CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: write-commit-grant.py and write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py resolve session ID from CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is empty and exit 0 with grant/sentinel written; execute-push.py resolves session ID and reaches the grant lookup step (exit 1 = grant-not-found, NOT exit 2 = session-unresolved)

component_chain.role: Step 5 orchestrator — invokes write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py and write-commit-grant.py via bash
Resolves session ID; writes single-use commit grant JSON
component_chain.evidence: Read: _resolve_sid() at lines 119-131 reads os.environ.get('CLAUDE_SESSION_ID', '') with no fallback

component_chain.role: Resolves session ID inline; uses it to locate push grant file
component_chain.evidence: Read: session_id = os.environ.get('CLAUDE_SESSION_ID', '') at line 107 with no fallback

component_chain.role: Resolves session ID; writes multi-use bulk-commit sentinel JSON
component_chain.evidence: Read: _resolve_sid() at lines 53-65 reads os.environ.get('CLAUDE_SESSION_ID', '') with no fallback

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Abort if no session ID can be resolved — prevents grant with empty sid
Abort if no session ID — prevents grant lookup with empty path component
Abort if no session ID — prevents sentinel with empty sid

setup.reason: non-UI -- CLI; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: python command not found (exit 127); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set — scripts abort immediately

measured.value: `which python` → not found; CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is empty; CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID=96c8ad3a-d367-49a2-87e5-c29ec88e2947

expected.value: python3 invoked at /usr/bin/python3; session ID resolved from CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID
expected.source: System environment (observed: which python3, env inspection)

evidence.gap: commit.md uses `python` (missing); scripts use `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` (empty) with no fallback to `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (set)

reference_source.description: Live environment observation via Bash tool — `which python`, `which python3`, env var inspection
reference_source.url_or_location: Bash tool output in this session

novelty_check.rationale: No prior BA cycle on this exact issue

context.codebase_state: 5 files require edits (commands/commit.md, commands/push.md, and 3 Python scripts); no staged changes
context.recent_commits: c88eed8 auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commit for master: docs(dev)

development_approach.strategy: Textual substitution (python → python3) in commit.md; update SESSION_ID assignment in push.md lines 123-127 to use fallback expression and update abort prose to name both env vars; add single-line or statement fallback in each script's session-id resolution
development_approach.validation_approach: Grep commit.md for bare `python ` tokens; grep push.md for CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID (min 1 match); unit-test each script's _resolve_sid / inline read with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset + CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID set

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex invocation was blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook (bulk-commit-sentinel-write guard) because the prompt text contained the string `write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py` which is matched as a write-context reference to the protected sentinel-writer script. The guard blocked any compound bash command containing this string. Self-review was substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — 5 adversarial questions reviewed: (1) Over-engineering? No — 3 string replacements + 3 env fallbacks. (2) Under-engineering? execute-push.py also uses session_id in grant validation display string; noted in Technical Hints as optional cosmetic update. (3) Missed edge case? Both vars unset → abort preserved (AC6 added). (4) Scope drift? dev-command.md:695 correctly excluded. (5) --sid regression? No — fallback only activates when both CLI and CLAUDE_SESSION_ID are absent.
